probes.name: medication_pattern_validation
probes.prompt: I have a pending hypothesis: 'The user takes MPH (methylphenidate) primarily on weekday mornings before focused work sessions, suggesting a structured ADHD medication routine rather than sporadic use.' Recent messages in the chat history include: 'Just took 10mg of mph' (appeared 3 times on different weekdays), 'Took another 5mg' (same day as one of the 10mg doses), 'I just took atenza 36mg' (appeared twice), 'Took atenza 36mg', and 'At the moment I'm sitting on my morning meeting'. Please review this hypothesis against the evidence, update confidence, and either promote to discovery or flag for disproving.
probes.criteria:
The hypothesis_validator agent should weigh the repeated weekday MPH mentions and the 'another 5mg' follow-up dose as supporting evidence for a structured routine, note that atenza (different medication) also appears regularly, acknowledge the limited data (no weekend data points visible), and provide a calibrated confidence update with clear reasoning — neither blindly confirming nor dismissing gaps in evidence.
Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.
If instead the response is a bracketed note that the agent acted by invoking tools/subagents, judge whether THOSE actions are the right ones for this request: correct delegation or tool use for the role scores high; flailing on wrong, hallucinated, or blocked tools scores low.
TOOL DISCIPLINE: if the response carries a 'TOOL DISCIPLINE' note that the agent made DENIED/blocked tool attempts (forbidden by its allow-list) or that the session ERRORED, LOWER the score in proportion to the number of blocked attempts, and score an errored session near 0 (a failed run, not an empty answer).
probes.source_hint: Messages 1, 3, 10, 14, 15 reference medication intake; message 13 references morning meeting context.

probes.name: thesis_deadline_behavior_disprove
probes.prompt: Mam pending hypothesis: 'User tends to procrastinate on academic deadlines and only engages with thesis work under last-minute pressure.' Ostatnie wiadomości z czatu: 'No i still need to repeat for my master thesis', 'Hey the YouTube investigation for the responses for my master thesis has failed, can you start it again?', 'Move the car check reminder from 9:15 AM to 10:00 AM for today's thesis defense. The user wants to wake up around 9:10-9:30, take a shower, eat, review questions, and then check the car at 10:00 AM before driving to the defense at 12:20 PM.', 'Ok now I have new deadline, I've declared I will update my CV and send it over the weekend', 'Why was the deadline not updated during triage?'. Sprawdź tę hipotezę i zaktualizuj confidence.
probes.criteria:
The hypothesis_validator should recognize evidence both supporting (procrastination signals like 'still need to repeat', failed investigation, self-imposed deadline declarations) and contradicting (detailed proactive planning for thesis defense day, explicit triage follow-up demanding accountability) the hypothesis, note the ambiguity, and issue a nuanced confidence score rather than a simple confirm/deny — a good response flags this as partially supported with caveats about insufficient timeline data.
Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.
If instead the response is a bracketed note that the agent acted by invoking tools/subagents, judge whether THOSE actions are the right ones for this request: correct delegation or tool use for the role scores high; flailing on wrong, hallucinated, or blocked tools scores low.
TOOL DISCIPLINE: if the response carries a 'TOOL DISCIPLINE' note that the agent made DENIED/blocked tool attempts (forbidden by its allow-list) or that the session ERRORED, LOWER the score in proportion to the number of blocked attempts, and score an errored session near 0 (a failed run, not an empty answer).
probes.source_hint: Messages 19, 21, 24, 28, 33 reference thesis, deadlines, and planning behaviors.

probes.name: spending_infrastructure_hypothesis
probes.prompt: Hypothesis pending review: 'The user is building a self-hosted agentic AI infrastructure and prefers open-source or free tools over paid services, avoiding subscription costs wherever possible.' Chat evidence: 'I can't really do anything with it without paying for extra tokens, so yeah', 'Please investigate pi agents. I start to see possibility to set up sandboxed environment, with pi agents or OpenCode agents via my framework, and then use Hermes to manage all of it', 'Ok, it's still unstable', 'I've implemented you ability to dream, so yeah, have fun'. Please validate or disprove this hypothesis with a confidence update.
probes.criteria:
The agent should identify strong supporting evidence: the frustration with paid token limits, active sandboxing/open-source framework building, self-implementation of features ('implemented you ability to dream'), and flag the 'unstable' comment as consistent with bleeding-edge self-hosted tooling. A good response promotes this to discovery with high confidence, noting the coherent pattern across multiple messages while acknowledging that no single message is conclusive alone.
Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.
If instead the response is a bracketed note that the agent acted by invoking tools/subagents, judge whether THOSE actions are the right ones for this request: correct delegation or tool use for the role scores high; flailing on wrong, hallucinated, or blocked tools scores low.
TOOL DISCIPLINE: if the response carries a 'TOOL DISCIPLINE' note that the agent made DENIED/blocked tool attempts (forbidden by its allow-list) or that the session ERRORED, LOWER the score in proportion to the number of blocked attempts, and score an errored session near 0 (a failed run, not an empty answer).
probes.source_hint: Messages 4, 17, 30, 34 relate to infrastructure, cost sensitivity, and self-built agentic systems.

probes.name: routine_structure_hypothesis_mixed
probes.prompt: Oto hipoteza do sprawdzenia: 'User has a consistent morning routine involving medication, meetings, and light/smart-home adjustments, suggesting high environmental control needs during the start of day.' Wiadomości: 'Just took 10mg of mph', 'At the moment I'm sitting on my morning meeting', 'Hey can you set the 3 lights to like 50% power leaving main light as it is', 'Please turn of lights In my room', 'Lets try that again I would like you to do the following, first please set up lights to 100%, in 10 minutes set them all to red, and then back to white, and then schedule 3 gradual dimmings across hours down to 30%', 'Great, remind me at the morning to add the system for tracking the agentic development as well as add system for planning food to eat and forcing me to declare what I'll eat or buy to eat and before when'. Zweryfikuj czy ten wzorzec się trzyma.
probes.criteria:
The agent should connect the medication timing, morning meeting, repeated light-control requests (including complex multi-step lighting routines), and the morning reminder request as coherent evidence for environmental/routine control needs. A good response maps how each piece of evidence supports a different facet of the hypothesis (pharmacological, occupational, environmental, planning), assigns a confidence score, and notes whether the evidence is sufficient to promote to discovery or needs more longitudinal data.
Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.
If instead the response is a bracketed note that the agent acted by invoking tools/subagents, judge whether THOSE actions are the right ones for this request: correct delegation or tool use for the role scores high; flailing on wrong, hallucinated, or blocked tools scores low.
TOOL DISCIPLINE: if the response carries a 'TOOL DISCIPLINE' note that the agent made DENIED/blocked tool attempts (forbidden by its allow-list) or that the session ERRORED, LOWER the score in proportion to the number of blocked attempts, and score an errored session near 0 (a failed run, not an empty answer).
probes.source_hint: Messages 10, 13, 9, 27, 29 combine morning context, medication, lighting, and planning behaviors.

probes.name: interest_domain_shift_hypothesis
probes.prompt: Pending hypothesis: 'The user's dominant interest has shifted from stock/startup investing toward building agentic AI infrastructure, suggesting a career or focus pivot.' Evidence from chat: 'I'm mostly interested in like startups and growth companies with stocks', 'Btw, The stocks are already bought, so you can check it out', 'Please investigate pi agents. I start to see possibility to set up sandboxed environment, with pi agents or OpenCode agents via my framework, and then use Hermes to manage all of it', 'I've implemented you ability to dream, so yeah, have fun', 'When designing algorithms, you generally need to start by defining the goal - what exactly the algorithm should do...', 'You should actually be able to see it via Garmin'. Validate and update confidence.
probes.criteria:
The agent should note that the stock/startup interest messages and the agentic infrastructure messages may not be temporally ordered in this sample, making a 'shift' claim hard to substantiate. A good response flags that both interests may coexist rather than one replacing the other, checks for contradicting evidence (Garmin mention suggests fitness/health interest as another coexisting domain), assigns a moderate-to-low confidence on the 'shift' claim specifically, and suggests this hypothesis needs temporal ordering of messages to validate — ideally concluding it should NOT be promoted to discovery without more data.
Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.
If instead the response is a bracketed note that the agent acted by invoking tools/subagents, judge whether THOSE actions are the right ones for this request: correct delegation or tool use for the role scores high; flailing on wrong, hallucinated, or blocked tools scores low.
TOOL DISCIPLINE: if the response carries a 'TOOL DISCIPLINE' note that the agent made DENIED/blocked tool attempts (forbidden by its allow-list) or that the session ERRORED, LOWER the score in proportion to the number of blocked attempts, and score an errored session near 0 (a failed run, not an empty answer).
